install.headline: Connect your first repository
install.subtext: CodeShield reviews every pull request automatically — catching bugs, security issues, and performance regressions before they reach production.

install.step2Desc: Push a branch and open a PR — CodeShield triggers automatically.
install.step3Desc: Specialist agents analyze your code and post inline comments within seconds.

landing.heroHeadline: Automated code review that reads your pull requests like a senior engineer.
landing.heroSubtext: CodeShield integrates directly into your GitHub workflow to provide deep technical insights, security catches, and performance optimizations before you merge.
landing.specialistsSubtitle: We deploy a swarm of specialized agents, each trained on specific engineering disciplines.
landing.securityItem1: Unsanitized user input in ORM queries
landing.performanceItem1: N+1 query patterns in data loops
landing.reviewSubtitle: Switch between specialists to see how they analyze a real-world code snippet.
landing.pathTraversalDesc: The application saves files using the original filename without validation. An attacker could use ../ to overwrite sensitive system files.
landing.pathTraversalFix: Use os.path.basename() or a secure filename generator...
landing.n1QueryDesc: Each loop iteration executes a separate database query. With 1000 users this results in 1001 queries. Use a JOIN or eager loading.
landing.layerViolationDesc: The controller directly imports and calls the email service, creating tight coupling. Use dependency injection via constructor parameters.
landing.layerViolationFix: Inject EmailService via constructor, call self._email.send()...
landing.archSubtitle: Built for speed, reliability, and security. We process reviews in seconds, not minutes.
landing.pricingSubtitle: Simple, predictable plans for projects of all sizes.
landing.ctaSubtext: Takes less than 2 minutes to connect your first repository.

hero.headline: AI-powered code review that thinks like a senior engineer.
hero.subtext: CodeShield deploys four parallel specialist agents on every pull request — catching security vulnerabilities, logic bugs, performance regressions, and style issues before they reach production.

stack.title: Tech Stack
stack.subtitle: Every technology chosen for a reason — nothing accidental, nothing redundant.

problem.title: The Problem
problem.subtitle: Manual code review doesn't scale.
problem.card1Desc: Pull requests sit for hours waiting for a free reviewer. Review bottlenecks extend cycle time and block deployment.
problem.card2Desc: Review quality varies by reviewer, time of day, and context size. The same bug may be caught today and missed tomorrow.
problem.card3Desc: Reviewers can't hold the full diff, dependency graph, security advisories, and performance characteristics in working memory simultaneously.

solution.title: The Solution
solution.subtitle: Specialist agents that never tire, never forget, and never miss context.
solution.point1Desc: Review starts within seconds of a push. The pipeline returns findings in under a minute for most pull requests.
solution.point2Desc: The same specialist expertise is applied to every PR, every time — regardless of team size or reviewer availability.
solution.point3Desc: Tool-equipped agents read adjacent files, fetch full diffs, and cross-reference context across the entire repository.

pipeline.title: How It Works
pipeline.subtitle: An end-to-end pipeline from GitHub webhook to Check Run annotation.
pipeline.fanoutDesc: The planner dispatches up to four specialists concurrently. Each runs an independent ReAct loop with up to 6 tool calls. A specialist failure never kills the run — it emits a failure event and returns an empty findings list so the aggregator keeps working.

specialists.title: Specialist Agents
specialists.subtitle: Each specialist is a ReAct agent with full tool access to the repository.
specialists.securityDesc: Identifies vulnerabilities, injection vectors, authentication flaws, and insecure cryptographic usage.
specialists.securityEx1: Unsanitized user input in ORM queries
specialists.correctnessDesc: Catches logic errors, race conditions, null-dereference risks, and off-by-one bugs.
specialists.performanceDesc: Flags N+1 query patterns, inefficient algorithms, memory leaks, and cache misuse.
specialists.performanceEx1: N+1 query patterns in data loops
specialists.styleDesc: Enforces architectural boundaries, detects circular dependencies, and flags naming inconsistencies.

decisions.title: Architecture Decisions
decisions.subtitle: Every technology choice is justified by a concrete engineering constraint.
decisions.langGraphDesc: Stateful graph with Annotated reducers for safe parallel fan-out. The Send API dispatches specialists dynamically based on the planner's output. Each node is isolated — a specialist failure emits a failure event and returns an empty findings list, keeping the run alive.
decisions.claudeDesc: Claude Sonnet for deep reasoning on code structure and security implications; Claude Haiku for lightweight classification and routing. Native tool use requires no adapter shims. Prompt caching cuts input token costs for repeated context.
decisions.langSmithDesc: Prompts are versioned in the Hub with semantic tags. Workers pull at boot and cache in memory. A bundled fallback guarantees zero-downtime prompt updates — the worker never fails because the Hub is unreachable.
decisions.supabaseDesc: Managed Postgres with pgvector, built-in GitHub OAuth, and Realtime postgres_changes — three services in one. Row-Level Security on every user-facing table. No WebSocket gateway to operate.
decisions.appRunnerDesc: Auto-scaled API with pay-per-request billing. No VPC, no ALB, no ECS task definition to maintain. Cold starts are acceptable for a webhook receiver — the 202 is returned before the Lambda even starts.
decisions.lambdaDesc: Per-invocation billing fits the bursty, unpredictable workload of PR reviews. Zero idle cost. Container image deployment reuses the same ECR image as the local dev environment.
decisions.sqsDesc: Durable queue with at-least-once delivery decouples the webhook endpoint (must respond in < 10s per GitHub's retry policy) from the LangGraph pipeline (30–90s execution). A dead-letter queue captures failures for post-mortem.
decisions.redisDesc: Two responsibilities: installation token cache (55-minute TTL, keyed by installation_id) and idempotency key store (24-hour TTL for X-GitHub-Delivery). Serverless billing means zero cost during idle periods.

infra.title: Infrastructure Philosophy
infra.subtitle: Pay-as-you-go over always-on. Every service bills only when it is doing work.
infra.card1Desc: The original design used Celery + Redis + ECS — three services running 24/7. Lambda replaced the worker entirely, dropping idle cost to $0.
infra.card2Desc: App Runner and Lambda run without a VPC, eliminating NAT Gateway costs (~$35/month minimum) and VPC management overhead — the single largest hidden cost in typical AWS deployments.
infra.card3Desc: Every AWS resource is defined in Terraform with remote state in S3 and DynamoDB locking. Infrastructure changes go through the same review process as code changes.
